values: Named artifacts and clear PM -> Coder -> QA -> Reviewer flow
Least privilege tools and explicit denials
Bounded repair path and TeamLog-style commitment
Weather, draft, incident, plan, and commitment artifacts use strict Pydantic-style contracts
Gateway, retry budget, A2A perimeter, or TeamLog boundary blocks unsafe behavior
Denied, repaired, or escalated paths leave inspectable evidence
Learner names the production failure each simple agent lab prevents
All handoffs use strict schemas rather than raw dictionaries
Invalid enums, extra fields, or missing fields produce Pydantic errors
Validated JSON output can be consumed by downstream agents
Visibility and sensitivity enforced before retrieval
Coder discovers hidden PM constraint through search
Connects memory to TeamLog and ToM
Allowed tools, trust tiers, and restricted data checks are enforced
Unauthorized calls are denied and logged with reasons
Learner explains least privilege and external boundary risks
Agentic RAG/MCP architecture with clear contracts
Policy gate, tool scopes, threat mitigations
Evidence that invalid and unauthorized actions fail safely
Identity, trust tiers, denied paths, auditability
Schemas and classification for cross-org messages
CEO prompt becomes ProjectPlan and TeamCommitment
High-risk work inserts SecurityReviewer before release
Contention is structured and escalates when unresolved
Coder reads updated commitment rather than raw CEO text
Approval, rejection, and needs_changes paths are auditable
End-to-end graph and strict Pydantic artifacts
Runs, fails once, repairs, and ships or escalates
Unauthorized tool and memory attempts are blocked
Bounded repair, route trace, useful failure memory
Release artifact traces back to validated work
Quality, test, security, and documentation agents emit strict artifacts
Assessments and generated artifacts are useful, specific, and grounded in patches
Strong, mediocre, unsafe, and edge-case examples are tested
Explains how skilled agents improve managed teams
High-risk, high-cost, low-confidence, and restricted actions require approval
Metrics compare configurations across accuracy, latency, cost, and hallucination rate
Caching, budget enforcement, retrieval, and forgetting are implemented
Network handoff uses strict OpenAPI schemas and API key security
Decisions are logged with enough evidence to debug
Container/API/config separation is secure and runnable
Logs, traces, metrics, and alerts expose workflow health
Untrusted code execution is isolated, bounded, and typed
Prompt/tool/model config versions can be compared and restored
